chairman: Mr Ian Holdsworth BSc MSc FRICS
decision_date: 10 January 2020 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 291 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 275.3 X 291 Minus Y 275.3 = (A) 15.7 (A) 15.7 Divided by Y 275.3 = (B) 0.057029 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.107029 Last registered rent* 165.00 Multiplied by (C) = 182.66 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 183.00 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £183.00 Per week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 5th October 2020 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 293.3 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 278.1 X 293.3 Minus Y 278.1 = (A) 15.2 (A) 15.2 Divided by Y 278.1 = (B) 0.5466 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 - NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.10466 Last registered rent* 181.50 Multiplied by (C) = 200.49 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 200.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £200.50 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

chairman: Mrs Evelyn Flint DMS FRICS IRRV
decision_date: Tuesday 21st April 2021 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 296.0 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 278.1 X 296.0 Minus Y 278.1 = (A) 17.9 (A) 17.9 Divided by Y 278.1 = (B) 0.06436 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) NO If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.11436 Last registered rent* £425.00 Multiplied by (C) = £473.60 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £474.00 Variable service charge YES / NO If YES add amount for services NO MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £474.00 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: Tuesday 25th May 2021 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 296.9 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 284.1 X 296.9 Minus Y 284.1 = (A) 12.8 (A) 12.8 Divided by Y 284.1 = (B) 0.04505 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 - NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) No If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.09505 Last registered rent* £160.00 Multiplied by (C) = 175.20 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £175.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £175.50 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 18 June 2021 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 301.1 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 275.3 X 301.1 Minus Y 275.3 = (A) 25.8 (A) 25.8 Divided by Y 275.3 = (B) 0.0937 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.1437 Last registered rent* 129.50 (no sc) Multiplied by (C) = 148.11 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 148.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £148.50 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 1 September 2021 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 305.5 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 278.1 X 305.5 Minus Y 278.1 = (A) 27.4 (A) 27.4 Divided by Y 278.1 = (B) 0.098525 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.14852571 Last registered rent* 155 Multiplied by (C) = 14760.71 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 178.5 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £178.5 Per week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). decision_date: 13th September 2021 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 305.5 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 278.3 X 305.5 Minus Y 278.3 = (A) 27.2 (A) 27.2 Divided by Y 278.3 = (B) 0.0977 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.1477 Last registered rent* £13,194 Multiplied by (C) = £15,142.75 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £15,143 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £15,143 Per Year Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 9th February 2022 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 317.7 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 291.9 X 317.7 Minus Y 291.9 = (A) 25.8 (A) 25.8 Divided by Y 291.9 = (B) 0.08838 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) No If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.13838 Last registered rent* £23,443.50 Multiplied by (C) = 26687.76 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £26,6688.00 Variable service charge YES If YES add amount for services £2,239.71 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £26,688.00 Per Year Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 20 July 2022 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 337.1 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y X 337.1 Minus Y 291.0 = (A) 46.1 (A) 46.1 Divided by Y 291.0 = (B) 0.1584 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.2084 Last registered rent* 141.66 (ex sc) Multiplied by (C) = 171.18 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 171.50 Variable service charge YES If YES add amount for services 13.35 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £184.53 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 30 November 2022 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 356.2 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 284.5 X 356.2 Minus Y 284.5 = (A) 71.7 (A) 71.7 Divided by Y 284.5 = (B) 0.2520 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) No If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.302 Last registered rent* £273.00 Multiplied by (C) = 355.45 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £355.50 Variable service charge YES If YES add amount for services £4.50 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £360.00 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 24th January 2023 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 358.3 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 256.3 X 358.3 Minus Y 256.3 = (A) 102 (A) 102 Divided by Y 256.3 = (B) 0.397971 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.447971 Last registered rent* £127.50 Multiplied by (C) = £184.62 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £185 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £185 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 18th May 2023 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 367.2 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 294.6 X 367.2 Minus Y 294.6 = (A) 72.6 (A) 72.6 Divided by Y 294.6 = (B) 0.246436 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.296436 Last registered rent* £174.50 Multiplied by (C) = £226.23 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £226.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services N/A MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £226.50 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. decision_date: 23 August 2023 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 376.4 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 304.0 X 376.4 Minus Y 304.0 = (A) 72.4 (A) 72.4 Divided by Y 304.0 = (B) 0.238158 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.288158 Last registered rent* £3078 Multiplied by (C) = £3,964.95 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £3,965 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services N/A MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £3,695 Per Quarter Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 26 September 2023 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 376.6 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 307.4 X 376.6 Minus Y 307.4 = (A) 69.2 (A) 69.2 Divided by Y 307.4 = (B) 0.225114 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.275114 Last registered rent* 26800.00 Multiplied by (C) = 34173.05 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 37173.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £ 34173.50 Per year Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 7 February 2023 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 360.4 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 293.50 X 360.4 Minus Y 293.50 = (A) 66.9 (A) 66.9 Divided by Y 293.50 = (B) 0.2279387 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.2779 Last registered rent* 7571 Multiplied by (C) = 9675.27 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 9675.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £9675.50 Per Year Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 19 February 2024 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 378 (Jan 2024, month before Tribunal Decision) PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 308.6 (Sept 2021, date of registration of previous rent) X 378 Minus Y 308.6 = (A) 69.40 (A) 69.40 Divided by Y 308.6 = (B) 0.225 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 0.225 + 1.05 = 1.275 Last registered rent* £141.50 Multiplied by (C) = £180.41 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £180.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £180.50 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent. FR24 SUMMARY REASONS Background 1. The Landlord applied to register a fair rent of £228 per week by application dated 28 August 2023 and the Rent Officer registered £180 per week on 18 October 2023 with effect from 19 November 2023. 2. The previous rent registered on 23 September 2021 (with effect from 19 November 2021) was £141.50 per week. 3. Following an objection from the Tenant (made by letter to the Valuation Office Agency on 27 October 2023) to the determination by the Rent Officer, the Tribunal has made a determination under the provisions of the Rent Act 1977. Inspection 4. The Tribunal did not inspect the property but considered this case on the basis of the papers provided by the parties. Evidence 5. There were no written submissions from the parties.  The Tribunal considered the documents that had been sent by the Valuation Office Agency, the Landlord and the Tenant. Determination and Valuation 6. The property is a Victorian terraced house. The accommodation comprises 2 rooms and 1 kitchen on the ground floor and 3 rooms and 1 bathroom/WC on the first floor. It has no central heating. 7. Having regard to our own expert, general knowledge of rental values in the area (including by reviewing local properties on Rightmove.co.uk) as well as the list of 36 comparables provided by the Landlord, we consider that the open market rent for the property in the condition and with the amenities it has would be around £460 per week. 8. This hypothetical rent is adjusted as necessary to allow for the differences between the terms and conditions considered usual for such a letting in today’s market and the condition of the actual property at the date of the determination under the Rent FR24 Act 1977.  Any benefit derived from the tenant’s improvements (if any) is disregarded. 9. The following deductions were made: Windows in poor order and no double glazing 10% Condition of bathroom/WC 5% Condition of kitchen 5% Condition of electrics 5% No central heating 10% No carpets & curtains provided by landlord 5% No decorating & internal repairing obligations on the landlord 10% Total 50% 10. That gave a fair rent of £230 per week (50% of £460 = £230). 11. A further deduction for scarcity of 20% was then made (20% of £230 = £46) giving an uncapped fair rent for the purposes of section 70 of the 1977 Act of £184 per week. Decision 12. The capped rent for the property according to the provisions of the Rent Acts (Maximum Fair Rent) Order 1999 is calculated at £180.50 per week (see calculation above). 13. In this case, the lower rent of £180.50 per week is to be registered as the fair rent for this property. Chairman:    Judge Rosanna Foskett, Mr Kevin Ridgeway MRICS Date: 19 February 2024 APPEAL PROVISIONS These summary reasons are provided to give the parties an indication as to how the Tribunal made its decision. If either party wishes to appeal this decision, they should first make a request for full reasons and the details of how to appeal will be set out in the full FR24 reasons. Any subsequent application for permission to appeal should be made on Form RP PTA.

decision_date: 19th March 2024 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 378 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 307.4 X 378 Minus Y 307.4 = (A) 70.6 (A) 70.6 Divided by Y 307.4 = (B) 0.23 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) 1.305 If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) Last registered rent* 792 Multiplied by (C) = 1033,56 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 1034.00 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £1034 Per Month Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 16 April 2024 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 381 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 314.3 X 381 Minus Y 314.3 = (A) 66.7 (A) 66.7 Divided by Y 314.3 = (B) 0.21222 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.26222 Last registered rent* 28600.00 Multiplied by (C) = 36099.42 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = 36100.00 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £36100.00 Per Year Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 10 May 2024 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 383.00 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 308.60 X 383.00 Minus Y 308.60 = (A) 74.40 (A) 74.40 Divided by Y 308.60 = (B) 0.241 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.291 Last registered rent* £5890 Multiplied by (C) = £7603.99 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £7604.00 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services n/a MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £ 7604.00 Per quarter Explanatory Note 1. decision_date: 25th June 2024 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE X 385 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE Y 275.1 X 385 Minus Y 275.1 = (A) 109.9 (A) 109.9 Divided by Y 275.1 = (B) .39949109 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 YES/NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.44949109 Last registered rent* £128.00 Multiplied by (C) = 1.44949109 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £185.50 Variable service charge NO If YES add amount for services N/A MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £185.50 Per Week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 17 December 2024 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE (2 months prior)   X 390.7 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE (2 months prior) Y 258.50 X 390.7 Minus Y 258.50 = (A) 132.20 (A) 132.20 Divided by Y 258.50 = (B) 0.5114 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.5644 Last registered rent* £154.50 pw (excl £9.14 pw sc) Multiplied by (C) = £226.97 pw *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £227 pw Variable service charge YES If YES add amount for services £13.05 pw MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £240.05 Per week Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.

decision_date: 13 January 2026 FR24 MAXIMUM FAIR RENT CALCULATION LATEST RPI FIGURE (2 months prior)   X 405.60 PREVIOUS RPI FIGURE (2 months prior) Y 292.60 X 405.60 Minus Y 292.60 = (A) 113 (A) 113 Divided by Y 292.60 = (B) 0.3862 First application for re-registration since 1 February 1999 NO If yes (B) plus 1.075 = (C) If no (B) plus 1.05 = (C) 1.4362 Last registered rent* £402.27 (ex £31.13sc) Multiplied by (C) = £577.74 *(exclusive of any variable service charge) Rounded up to nearest 50p = £577.75 pcm Variable service charge Yes If YES add amount for services £99.12 pcm MAXIMUM FAIR RENT = £676.86 Per pcm Explanatory Note 1. The calculation of the maximum fair rent, in accordance with the formula contained in the Order, is set out above. 2. In summary, the formula provides for the maximum fair rent to be calculated by: (a) increasing the previous registered rent by the percentage change in the retail price index (the RPI) since the date of that earlier registration and (b) adding a further 7.5% (if the present application was the first since 1 February 1999) or 5% (if it is a second or subsequent application since that date). A 7.5% increase is represented, in the calculation set out above, by the addition of 1.075 to (B) and an increase of 5% is represented by the addition of 1.05 to (B). The result is rounded up to the nearest 50 pence. 3. For the purposes of the calculation the latest RPI figure (x) is that published in the calendar month immediately before the month in which the Tribunal’s fair rent determination was made. 4. The process differs where the tenancy agreement contains a variable service charge and the rent is to be registered as variable under section 71(4) of the Rent Act 1977. In such a case the variable service charge is removed before applying the formula. When the amount determined by the application of the formula is ascertained the service charge is then added to that sum in order to produce the maximum fair rent.
